items.title: Parents rally to save 'soul of Brunswick' as youth centre goes up for sale

children.content: Parents in Melbourne's inner north are calling on the state government to save Brunswick's Don Bosco youth centre, which is set to be sold after nine decades.

gallery.altText: One child looks to the side while another grins at the camera as a female carer looks on.

thumbnail.altText: One child looks to the side while another grins at the camera as a female carer looks on.

items.title: Bondi attack had traumatic effect on MP's son, royal commission hears

children.content: Liberal MP Julian Leeser tells the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion that his young son became too scared to attend Jewish events after last year's Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: Julian Leeser Liberal MP for Berowra stands outdoors and looks at the camera

items.title: Edmund Rice Education to pay out victim-survivors of Christian Brothers

children.content: Victim-survivors of abuse by members of the Christian Brothers organisation will be able to receive compensation for civil claims under a new payout proposal.

image.altText: A necklace with a wooden church cross and Jesus on the cross sits on a table.

items.title: Man linked to Isla Bell death fails to have case thrown out

children.content: Marat Ganiev was seeking a permanent stay on his case, with his lawyer arguing the multiple changes to the prosecution case were an abuse of process.

image.altText: Photo of a young woman with red hair and nose piercings

items.title: Have the AFL's rule changes worked? Not exactly

children.content: A raft of AFL rule changes for the 2026 season have either excited or horrified a range of fans — Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson examine the changes and whether they have had the desired impact.

image.altText: A Fremantle AFL ruckman leaps high to tap the ball as an Essendon opponent stands watching.

items.title: Thousands living 'as Australians in Australia' without a nationality

children.content: As a Rohingya person from Myanmar, Asma was denied an official nationality. Migration experts say statelessness, an issue faced by over 8,000 people in Australia, remains poorly understood.

gallery.altText: Asma, wearing a brown headscarf and glasses, stares into the distance in front of a glass window

thumbnail.altText: Asma, wearing a brown headscarf and glasses, stares into the distance in front of a glass window

items.title: Fire brigade pushes for compensation after 22 members diagnosed with cancer

children.content: In Nar Nar Goon's fire brigade, 22 volunteers have been diagnosed with cancer. But as members work to obtain their work insurance cover, almost half of the members are not covered by the state's presumptive legislation.

image.altText: Two firefighters, and older man and a woman, standing side by side

items.title: Big Build crime and corruption allegations warrant inquiry, says expert

children.content: One of Australia's leading public policy academics says a "Queensland-style" commission of inquiry is needed into Victoria's Big Build.

image.altText: the wind blows balck and yellow flags with yellow writing attached to fences of building sites with buildings behind

items.title: Elite boarding school review uncovers 'normalised' abuse

children.content: An independent review of an elite private school in regional Victoria describes a culture of "normalised" racism, bullying and abuse.

gallery.altText: a sign of Ballarat Grammar school

thumbnail.altText: a sign of Ballarat Grammar school

items.title: 'Prehistoric' hurdles keep this club's women's program from thriving

children.content: Macedon Football Netball Club is proud to do things its way. But it is in a pinch with its women and girls team.

image.altText: A woman with a scarf around her neck, on a dark sports field.

items.title: Fast food key to farmers making more with their dairy calves

children.content: Dairy beef has caught the attention of fast food companies as farmers beef up bobby calves into viable assets.

image.altText: A dairy farmer is surrounded by young calves

items.title: 'Terribly expensive for no service': Claims new mobile maps still not accurate

children.content: Telcos were forced to publish mobile phone coverage maps to new standards last month, but some Telstra customers say they still do not reflect reality.

image.altText: Man holding a mobile phone to his earn sits in a chair on his front porch with a dog at his feet

items.title: 'Fascinating world': McRae questions booing directed at Quaynor

children.content: Collingwood franks its strong run of form with an impressive 34-point win over Adelaide, as Crows fans defy pleas from the club and boo Isaac Quaynor.

image.altText: GettyImages-2286661073

items.title: Protestors say Victorian teacher rally a 'watershed moment'

children.content: More than 20,000 state school teachers and support staff rallied in Melbourne's CBD and around Victoria after walking off the job following the rejection of a government pay increase.

gallery.altText: A large crowd of people in front of Parliament House in Melbourne protesting as part of teacher industrial action.
Bright banners and signs in a crowd of people.
Someone holds a sign that says, 'Government report card (F)'.

thumbnail.altText: A large crowd of people in front of Parliament House in Melbourne protesting as part of teacher industrial action.
Bright banners and signs in a crowd of people.
Someone holds a sign that says, 'Government report card (F)'.

items.title: Antisemitism 'using Zionism as an excuse', rabbi tells royal commission

children.content: A recent wave of antisemitism has been far-reaching, even targeting Jewish people who are critical of the Israeli government, a royal commission probing the phenomenon has heard.

image.altText: A woman standing.

items.title: Driver boasted of drink driving, weeks before killing 12-year-old girl

children.content: County Court judge Michael Tinney says Jordan Kenyon's decision to get behind the wheel after consuming the drug GHB was like playing "Russian roulette" with the general public.

image.altText: Girl wearing glasses puts her arm around her father at a kitchen table.

items.title: Melbourne football team to play without crowd after sexist fan conduct

children.content: The Preston Lions, associated with the Macedonian Australian community, will be forced to play without spectators after some of its fans displayed offensive banners at a game last weekend.

image.altText: A man in red soccer attire runs towward a ball during a match.

items.title: Record $60k fine for man behind 'brazen' dump site that will cost $1m to clean

children.content: The owner of one of Victoria's worst illegal dump sites has been handed a record fine of $60,000 and ordered to pay an expected $1 million to clean up the polluted property in Melbourne's outer north-east.

image.altText: A large pile of rubbish at a dump with smoke on a regional property

items.title: Parks Victoria proposes closure of about 100 Arapiles climbs

children.content: A proposed draft amendment of a park management plan, first written in 1991, would close about 100 climbing routes, while more than 3,000 others would remain open.

gallery.altText: A mountain behind a lake on a cloudy day.

thumbnail.altText: A mountain behind a lake on a cloudy day.

items.title: Proposed $200m timber hub promises jobs and building materials boost

children.content: The timber manufacturing hub would transform blue gum wood chips into timber beams for construction.

image.altText: A control room mockup with people in it

items.title: Bridge operator opts to paint over rather than wash off Pam the Bird

children.content: A multi-day operation to paint over a Pam the Bird image on Melbourne's Bolte Bridge has begun, with Transurban painters abseiling from the top of a 140-metre-tall pylon.

image.altText: Man sits at the top of the Bolte Bridge pylon with a chicken illustration

items.title: Rock Posters proud to capture artists' 'blood and tears' in billboards

children.content: An iconic Melbourne printing factory has worked with international pop divas and rock legends to enhance the city's live music scene for 40 years.

image.altText: Two men standing in front of live music posters in the printing factory

items.title: Opposition promises to build new West Gippsland hospital if elected

children.content: The Victorian Coalition pledges $850 million to build a new West Gippsland hospital if it wins the November election.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a hospital nestled in green space.

items.title: Jewish community group details 'unprecedented' security needs

children.content: Police engagement failed to engender a sense of safety in Melbourne's Jewish community in the face of rising "vitriol", the antisemitism royal commission has heard.

articles.title: How school is different for Jewish Australian kids

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

image.altText: Rows of empty black desks, each with a white booklet on top and either brown or cream seats in a green room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a blue blazer stands in front of a Royal Commission sign.

items.title: AFL's first publicly gay player calls for respect in Crows-Pies match

children.content: Four months after becoming the first current or former AFL player to publicly come out as gay, Leigh Ryswyk is urging fans not to boo the Collingwood player targeted by Crow Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

image.altText: Leigh Ryswyk Header image

items.title: Woman in businessman rape case told 'lie after lie', defence says

children.content: A woman who accused a well-connected Melbourne businessman of rape is a serial liar and made "false allegations" in court, defence lawyers say.

image.altText: The exterior of the County Court of Victoria in Melbourne.

items.title: Grass 'skeletons' hint at 25,000-year-old Indigenous fire ritual in cave

children.content: Evidence of ancient burnt grasses unearthed in a Victorian cave suggest the old ancestors of the GunaiKurnai people used the cave for fire rituals during the Ice Age.

image.altText: GunaiKurnai Elder Uncle Russell Mullett at the cave entrance of Cloggs Cave

items.title: Drink spiking charge against Melbourne socialite Amy Tossoun thrown out

children.content: A Victorian magistrate has struck out a drink spiking charge against Melbourne socialite Amy Tossoun.

image.altText: A woman wearing an outlandish hat

items.title: Melbourne suburbs with 'one way out' where 86pc of people leave for work

children.content: With only months to the Victorian election, Melton Council is calling on both sides of politics to commit to two new train stations in the fast-growing municipality.

gallery.altText: Aerial photo of a full car park near a train station with cars parked along the roadside.

thumbnail.altText: Aerial photo of a full car park near a train station with cars parked along the roadside.

items.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries

children.content: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

image.altText: Woman at airport

items.title: Queensland mayors take disaster funding fight to Melbourne

children.content: Mayors from parts of Queensland hit hard by floods and cyclones have protested in a Melbourne park against planned changes to federal disaster funding.

image.altText: LGAQ protest

items.title: 'Surreal' sense of grief at packed town meeting after alleged Layla Jeffery murder

children.content: Victoria Police and the Buloke Shire Council have held an event updating 300 Donald community members on the alleged murder of Layla Jeffery.

items.title: Dairy giant Lactalis to close Longwarry factory by 2027

children.content: Lactalis has announced it will close its dairy factory in Longwarry, in Gippsland, with a phased transition of production to other sites.

image.altText: An exterior of the Longwarry dairy factory

items.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error

children.content: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

image.altText: Michelle Buckley stands indoors, dressed in a warm cardigan.

items.title: Synagogue leader says security bids knocked back before firebombing

children.content: Jewish leaders have given evidence to the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion regarding security concerns for synagogues nationwide.

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion is this week focused on security for the Australian Jewish community.

articles.title: Labor MP calls for exclusion zones at synagogues akin to abortion clinics

children.content: Former attorney-general Mark Dreyfus has pushed for more robust social media moderation and protest laws at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

articles.title: Holocaust museum staff and volunteers targeted by hateful comments

children.content: Melbourne Holocaust Museum has had to step up security since 2020 following a rise in incidents that have seen threatening voice messages and hateful comments left online.
